claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Excellence section B.1.1 states 'seven specific, measurable, and achievable objectives' but Tier 3 objectives.json defines 8 objectives (OBJ-7 'Open framework release and ecosystem contribution' is absent from the section's enumeration)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json (defines 8 objectives: OBJ-1, OBJ-2, OBJ-3, OBJ-4, OBJ-5, OBJ-6, OBJ-7, OBJ-8; OBJ-7 title='Open framework release and ecosystem contribution', responsible_partner='BAL', target_month=48 — present in file but absent from excellence section enumeration); excellence section B.1.1 content (enumerates OBJ-1, OBJ-2, OBJ-3, OBJ-8, OBJ-4, OBJ-5, OBJ-6 only, claiming count of 'seven')
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Excellence section B.1.1 claims 'seven specific, measurable, and achievable objectives' but docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json defines 8 objectives (OBJ-1 through OBJ-8). OBJ-7 ('Open framework release and ecosystem contribution', BAL lead, M48) is absent from the enumeration entirely. The stated count 'seven' and the omission of OBJ-7 both contradict the Tier 3 ground truth. This inconsistency is also documented in assembled draft consistency_log CC-01.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Impact section B.2.1 maps OBJ-6 to KPI-03 as '≥20% disruption recovery improvement' only — the conjunctive second metric '≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence' is absent from the impact section
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json (OBJ-6.measurable_target: 'Demonstrate ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time AND ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence compared to current planning systems, validated on ELI operational scenarios across 3 supply chain corridors' — two conjunctive quantified targets); impact section B.2.1 content (KPI-03 defined as '≥20% disruption recovery improvement (KPI-03)' — second metric absent from KPI-03 and not present elsewhere in impact section)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Impact section B.2.1 presents only half of OBJ-6's conjunctive measurable target. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json OBJ-6.measurable_target specifies both '≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time AND ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence.' The '≥15% delivery schedule adherence' metric is absent from KPI-03 and does not appear elsewhere in the impact section, creating a partial representation that contradicts the complete Tier 3 ground truth. Note: excellence section B.1.1 correctly states both metrics for OBJ-6. This inconsistency is documented in assembled draft consistency_log CC-06.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Implementation section B.3.2 cites ELI as 'EuroLog International' — the registered legal entity suffix 'AG' is absent, contradicting the Tier 3 canonical legal name
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json (ELI legal_name='EuroLog International AG' — suffix 'AG' is part of the registered legal name); implementation section B.3.2 content (cites 'EuroLog International' without the 'AG' suffix)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Implementation section B.3.2 uses 'EuroLog International' for partner ELI, omitting the registered legal entity suffix 'AG'. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json records legal_name as 'EuroLog International AG'. The truncated form does not match the canonical Tier 3 legal name. This inconsistency is documented in assembled draft consistency_log CC-03.

claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json (BAL legal_name='Boreal AI Labs Oy' — suffix 'Oy' is part of the registered legal name); implementation section B.3.2 content (cites 'Boreal AI Labs' without the 'Oy' suffix)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Implementation section B.3.2 uses 'Boreal AI Labs' for partner BAL, omitting the registered legal entity suffix 'Oy'. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json records legal_name as 'Boreal AI Labs Oy'. The truncated form does not match the canonical Tier 3 legal name. This inconsistency is documented in assembled draft consistency_log CC-03.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Impact section B.2.1 and B.2.2 characterise D4-01 as a standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE due by M48; Tier 4 wp_structure.json defines D4-01 as 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification' due at M18 — a research deliverable, not a standardisation submission
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (WP4 deliverable D4-01: title='Multi-agent coordination protocol specification', due_month=18 — confirmed in docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json MGR-WPL-WP4 responsibilities); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/impacts.json (IMP-6.linked_deliverable_ids=['D4-01'] for standardisation pathway); impact section B.2.1 and B.2.2 content (describes D4-01 as standardisation submission by M48)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The same deliverable ID D4-01 is assigned materially different purpose and timing across sections. wp_structure.json defines D4-01 as 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification' due at M18 (a research output). The impact section describes D4-01 as the formal standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48. While impacts.json IMP-6 links D4-01 to the standardisation pathway, the characterisation of D4-01 itself as a standardisation submission (rather than the research protocol that later gets submitted) and the timing discrepancy (M18 research output vs M48 standardisation submission) constitute a material cross-section inconsistency. This is documented in assembled draft consistency_log CC-04.
